track_label: Background international fleet renewal efficiency drift
track_description: Exogenous efficiency improvement from background replacement of A380 and B747 era aircraft with A350 and B787 generation (15-20% efficiency gain). Rate: -0.4%/yr compounding; multiplier 1.000 → 0.905 by 2050. Applies to conventional jet and SAF blend states.
evidence_summary: Airbus A350 vs A380 efficiency data; Boeing 787 vs 747 comparison; ICAO long-run fleet efficiency trend; BITRE historical intensity analysis.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2023-24 international aviation bunkers (165 PJ) / BITRE 70,000 million_pkm = 2357.0 GJ/million_pkm. NGGI check: 168.5 tCO2e/million_pkm × 70,000 / 1e6 = 11.8 MtCO2e.
double_counting_guardrail: Applies to conventional_jet and saf_blend only. Hydrogen aircraft excluded as it represents a distinct technology pathway. Non-stacking with aerodynamic retrofit package.
review_notes: Rate of -0.4%/yr is conservative relative to historical data; review against ICAO fleet-mix projections.
